The following sword-drinks have been laid down by Sukra-
carya (preceptor of the Demons) : One wishing for extra-ordinary
opulence should give a drink of blood to the sword; for begetting a
virtuous son, a drink of ghee (sacrificial offerings?); for inexhaustible
wealth, one of water; for acquiring great wealth by sinful means,

Signs oj Swords L 431
one of the milk of mare, camel or elephant; and for cutting the
trunks of elephants (in battles), one of fish-bile, and the milk of the
deer, horse and goat, mixed with the toddy of palm trees.

unguent prepared with the milky juice ol Arka plant mixed with
